senses.definition: to give someone a mild warning or punish them in a way that is not harsh

examples.x: The boss often slaps employees on the wrist for minor mistakes.
They slap him on the wrist every time he arrives late to work.
The teacher slaps students on the wrist when they forget their homework.

senses.definition: to make someone face the consequences or legal repercussions of their actions
to accept and confront the punishment or consequence of one's wrongdoings or irresponsible actions

examples.x: Despite his anxiety, he knows he must face the music and deliver the presentation.
She decides to face the music and have an honest conversation with her partner.
After causing a commotion at the party, he faced the music and apologized to everyone.
Despite feeling nervous, she faced the music and confronted her fear of public speaking.
Tomorrow, he will have to face the music and address the consequences of his actions.

senses.definition: to be deserving of something that happens to one, particularly something that is unpleasant

examples.x: She has it coming if she continues to cheat on her partner.
They had it coming for ignoring the warnings and causing the accident.
She will have it coming if she doesn't apologize for her hurtful remarks.
He was having it coming when he was caught stealing from the company.
They will be having it coming when the consequences of their actions catch up with them.

senses.definition: to take the blame for another person's actions or accept full responsibility for a crime or wrongdoing that other people were also part of

examples.x: He takes the fall for his team's mistake to protect their reputation.
They take the fall for the company's financial scandal to preserve the image of the organization.
He took the fall for his brother's crime and served time in prison.
She took the fall for the failed project, accepting the blame on behalf of the team.
They took the fall for the political scandal and faced public scrutiny.
He was brave enough to take the fall, accepting the punishment without hesitation.

senses.definition: something extremely hard that one goes through, often as a punishment

examples.x: I understand that you are sorry for your actions, but there is no need to wear a hair shirt because of it.
Leonard's putting on the hair shirt while he waits this thing out.
Oh, it's my punishment—my hair shirt, if you will—for blowing off my schoolwork this week.
He is up the river, serving a 10-year sentence for his involvement in the robbery.

senses.definition: used for saying someone is being held, detained, or imprisoned indefinitely at the will of the ruling British monarch

word_list.pronunciation: sˈuː ðə pˈænts ˈɔf ʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to take legal action against someone and demand so much money and damages from them that it leaves them with nothing

examples.x: She is determined to sue the pants off the company that plagiarized her book.
He is considering legal action and plans to sue the pants off the landlord for the unsafe living conditions.
They are currently in the process of suing the pants off their former employer for wrongful termination.
She sued the pants off the negligent driver and won a substantial settlement.
She will sue the pants off the online retailer if they fail to deliver the promised refund.
He intends to sue the pants off the hospital for the medical malpractice he experienced.

senses.definition: a prisoner who is sentenced to death and is about to be executed

examples.x: The condemned prisoner is a dead man walking as he awaits his scheduled execution.
He is fully aware that he is a dead man walking after being given the terminal diagnosis.
The explorer, lost in the wilderness for days, had felt like a dead man walking until he was finally rescued.
As he walked the final steps from his cell to the execution chamber, the condemned man knew he was a dead man walking, resigned to the inevitable end awaiting him.

senses.definition: to officially punish someone by death
to formally confront someone and demand an explanation for their conduct and actions, especially those in positions of authority who are deemed responsible

word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌnz hˈɛd ˌɑːn ɐ plˈeɪt plˈæɾɚ

senses.definition: used to refer to a punishment that is very harsh for a crime or wrong deed

examples.x: The politician knows that if the corruption scandal is exposed, they could end up with their head on a plate.
If the company's financial misconduct is discovered, the CEO could find their head on a platter.
The employee is treading on thin ice; if they make another major mistake, they might end up with their head on a platter.
After the embezzlement was uncovered, the executive had their head on a plate and was swiftly removed from the company.
The consequences of their actions are catching up to them; soon they will have their head on a platter, facing the fallout.

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈæp ˌɛsbˈiː ˌɑːn ˌoʊvɚɹ əkɹˌɑːs ðə nˈʌkəlz

senses.definition: to give someone a mild punishment for a mistake or wrongdoing

examples.x: The supervisor rapped me on the knuckles for submitting the report with errors.
My parents rapped me on the knuckles when I broke the vase by accident.
If they continue to neglect their responsibilities, their supervisor will rap them on the knuckles.

senses.definition: a situation where there will be severe consequences or difficulties as a result of certain actions or events

examples.x: There's the devil to pay if you don't finish your assignment on time.
They know there will be the devil to pay if they don't address the safety issues in the workplace.
She's realizing there's the devil to pay for getting involved in that risky business venture.
He discovered there was the devil to pay when his secret was exposed.
They're realizing there's the devil to pay for their ongoing negligence.

senses.definition: a specific amount of money that is offered as a reward to anyone who captures or kills a particular person

examples.x: There is currently a price on his head for his involvement in the criminal organization.
She knows that there's a price on her head, so she's constantly on the move to stay safe.
The fugitive is living in constant fear with a price on his head, always looking over his shoulder.
He narrowly escaped capture when there was a price on his head, thanks to his quick thinking.
The rebel leader was killed by mercenaries when there had been a price on his head for years.
Despite the danger, he managed to survive the time when there was a price on his head.
If he continues his illicit activities, there will be a price on his head, and he'll become a wanted criminal.
As tensions rise, more individuals will have a price on their heads, leading to an escalating conflict.
He's living in constant fear because there's a price on his head, and he's being hunted down.
